texto: My mother used to say not sleeping was the sign of a guilty mind. It could have been. There was a lot in my mind to feel guilty about. When you’re drunk and trying to sleep, your thoughts are visited by the ghosts of those deeds whose heat still glows hottest in your personal darkness. Our actions burn much longer than the moments in which they occur. And drunks like me, we hide from the glow of the embers by fueling other fires and hiding within the flames.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robert E. Dunn: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “My mother used to say not sleeping was the sign of a guilty mind. It could have been. There was a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, drunkness, fire pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e fire.
